message:
feat(abi): port 8 libcrypt aliases + DES no-op stubs + crypt_preferred_method + crypt_checksalt (bd-xapiq)

Eight libcrypt entries that close part of the libcrypt parity gap.
Our existing crypt() already supports MD5 / SHA-256 / SHA-512; these
add the historical aliases + DES no-ops + utility queries that
programs commonly link against without actually invoking crypto:

  - fcrypt(key, salt) — historical alias of crypt.
  - xcrypt(key, salt) — alternate-name alias of crypt.
  - encrypt(block, edflag) — DES single-block; no-op stub (DES is
    obsolete and our crypt rejects it).
  - encrypt_r(block, edflag, *data) — reentrant encrypt; no-op.
  - setkey(key) — DES key schedule; no-op.
  - setkey_r(key, *data) — reentrant setkey; no-op.
  - crypt_preferred_method() -> *const c_char — return a static
    "$6$" string (SHA-512 = the strongest method our crypt honors).
  - crypt_checksalt(setting) -> int — recognize $1$/$5$/$6$ prefixes
    and return CRYPT_SALT_OK = 0; everything else (and NULL) returns
    CRYPT_SALT_INVALID = 1 per libxcrypt convention.

Tests: 5 abi tests covering fcrypt/xcrypt alias equality with crypt
for both SHA-512 and MD5 inputs, encrypt/setkey no-op safety,
crypt_preferred_method static string, and crypt_checksalt
classification of all three known prefixes plus bogus/DES-2-char/NULL
rejection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
